company.name: Alnylam Pharmaceuticals
company.subtitle: Amvuttra $2.3B (+103%) ATTR-CM blockbuster | 2026 guide $4.9-5.3B | Alnylam 2030: 25%+ CAGR
company.cashRunway: Cash generative ($3B revenue, first profitable year 2025)

market.notes: Amvuttra 2025: $2.31B (+141%). ATTR-CM blockbuster after HELIOS-B (36% all-cause mortality reduction). Q4: $827M ($3.3B annualized). 2026 TTR guide: $4.4-4.7B. Onpattro declining ($173M). Total TTR 2026: 83% growth.

us.patientsK.note: Pfizer/BridgeBio 10-K 2024: ~120K US ATTR-CM (wild-type + hereditary), growing ~20%/yr post-tafamidis label expansion and cardiac scintigraphy awareness.

eu.patientsK.note: ESC 2023 ATTR registry + Pfizer EU: ~90K diagnosed/diagnosable ATTR-CM across EU5+UK; under-diagnosed vs prevalence estimates of 300K+.

row.patientsK.note: Alnylam/Pfizer ROW: ~60K treatable ATTR-CM (Japan high V30M hATTR endemic pockets + AU/CA commercial markets).

us.wtpPct.note: ~70% -- Medicare Part B/D covers Vyndaqel, Attruby, Amvuttra post-ATTR-ACT/HELIOS-B; commercial payors require scintigraphy confirmation.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~55% -- NICE/HAS reimburse tafamidis for NYHA I-II only; G-BA negotiated price cuts limiting access to severe genotypes.

row.wtpPct.note: ~40% -- Japan NHI covers tafamidis (V30M endemic); emerging Asia ex-Japan limited access.

eu.priceK.note: EU net ~$140K post HTA negotiation; UK NICE list $130K, Germany AMNOG ~$150K.

row.priceK.note: $90K blended: Japan NHI tafamidis ~$120K, emerging markets tiered ~$40-60K.

penPct.note: ~45% peak -- diagnosis rate expansion + HELIOS-B Amvuttra label unlock drives sustained uptake through 2030.

us.reachPct.note: Amvuttra+Onpattro 30% -- post-HELIOS-B CM label Mar 2025 expanded to ATTR-CM ~150K US eligible; transitioning many from Onpattro IV to Amvuttra SC q3mo.

us.priceK.note: Amvuttra WAC $476K/yr; Onpattro $450K; net ~$475K w/ outcomes-based contracts + Alnylam Patient Access.

us.wtpPct.note: 70% -- Medicare Part B (Amvuttra SC in-office) + commercial; HELIOS-B all-cause mortality drove payor broadening 2025.

eu.reachPct.note: 22% EU ~100K ATTR-CM -- EMA Amvuttra CM label 2024; NICE TA868 + G-BA added benefit proven HELIOS-B.

eu.wtpPct.note: 55% -- NICE/G-BA recommended; ATTR hematology/cardiology centers; ASMR III.

eu.priceK.note: $300K EU net -- UK NHS ~GBP 7.5K/mo, Germany ~EUR 10K/mo post-AMNOG; ~60% of US.

row.reachPct.note: 8% of 400K ROW ATTR -- Japan NHI Amvuttra 2023, AU PBS, Brazil/LatAm via Alnylam access.

row.wtpPct.note: 18% -- Japan NHI 30% copay major share, AU PBS, EM access program.

row.priceK.note: $120K blended -- Japan NHI ~$220K, AU ~$150K, EM tiered.

market.notes: Next-gen TTR silencer. Phase 3 TRITON-CM and TRITON-PN advancing. Potentially better knockdown + less frequent dosing. Extends TTR franchise beyond Amvuttra patents.

us.patientsK.note: Pfizer/BridgeBio 10-K 2024: ~120K US ATTR-CM (wild-type + hereditary), growing ~20%/yr post-tafamidis label expansion and cardiac scintigraphy awareness.

eu.patientsK.note: ESC 2023 ATTR registry + Pfizer EU: ~90K diagnosed/diagnosable ATTR-CM across EU5+UK; under-diagnosed vs prevalence estimates of 300K+.

row.patientsK.note: Alnylam/Pfizer ROW: ~60K treatable ATTR-CM (Japan high V30M hATTR endemic pockets + AU/CA commercial markets).

us.wtpPct.note: ~70% -- Medicare Part B/D covers Vyndaqel, Attruby, Amvuttra post-ATTR-ACT/HELIOS-B; commercial payors require scintigraphy confirmation.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~55% -- NICE/HAS reimburse tafamidis for NYHA I-II only; G-BA negotiated price cuts limiting access to severe genotypes.

row.wtpPct.note: ~40% -- Japan NHI covers tafamidis (V30M endemic); emerging Asia ex-Japan limited access.

eu.priceK.note: EU net ~$140K post HTA negotiation; UK NICE list $130K, Germany AMNOG ~$150K.

row.priceK.note: $90K blended: Japan NHI tafamidis ~$120K, emerging markets tiered ~$40-60K.

penPct.note: ~45% peak -- diagnosis rate expansion + HELIOS-B Amvuttra label unlock drives sustained uptake through 2030.

us.reachPct.note: Nucresiran (ALN-TTRsc04) Phase 3 TRITON-CM; 15% projected -- quarterly SC TTR silencing; filing 2027+; competing w/ Amvuttra itself.

us.priceK.note: Projected WAC ~$450K/yr -- parity to Amvuttra; less frequent dosing value prop.

us.wtpPct.note: 50% -- pre-approval; cannibalizes Amvuttra; Alnylam lifecycle management; payor tier specialty.

eu.reachPct.note: 10% EU ATTR -- EMA post-Ph3 2028+; transitions from Amvuttra.

eu.wtpPct.note: 38% -- HTA will require non-inferiority vs Amvuttra + convenience premium justification.

eu.priceK.note: $280K EU net projected -- parity to Amvuttra EU.

row.reachPct.note: 3% ROW -- Japan NHI + AU PBS pathway, EM via Alnylam.

market.notes: Twice-yearly injection for hypertension. ZENITH Phase 3 CVOT with Roche. If positive: $5-10B+ market. RNAi into largest CV market in the world.

us.patientsK.note: CDC 2024: ~120M US adults with HTN; ~10M resistant/uncontrolled on 3+ agents (Alnylam zilebesiran KARDIA target population).

eu.patientsK.note: ESC 2023: ~100M EU HTN; ~8M resistant HTN eligible for AGT siRNA or novel MOA add-on.

row.patientsK.note: WHO Global HTN 2023: ~1.3B HTN globally; ~60M resistant HTN ex-US/EU treated pool.

us.wtpPct.note: ~25% -- Medicare/commercial cover generic antihypertensives; novel siRNA (zilebesiran) will face step-edits to 3+ generics first.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~15% -- NICE/G-BA stringent on novel HTN pricing given abundant generics; resistant HTN niche.

row.wtpPct.note: ~5% -- Japan NHI conservative on novel HTN; emerging markets generic-dominant.

us.priceK.note: $8K/yr anchored to Alnylam zilebesiran estimated launch (biannual SC siRNA, premium vs generics $100).

eu.priceK.note: EU net ~$4K post HTA negotiation for resistant HTN niche.

row.priceK.note: $2K blended -- limited novel RNAi access ex-Japan.

penPct.note: ~8% peak -- resistant HTN subset realistic capture; KARDIA-3 Ph3 readout 2025 gates uptake.

us.reachPct.note: Zilebesiran (Roche partnership) Ph2 KARDIA; 1.2% of massive hypertension TAM -- Ph2 positive but novel chronic HTN RNAi approach; launch 2028+.

us.priceK.note: Projected WAC ~$8K/yr -- twice-yearly SC dosing; premium vs generic HTN meds but resistant subpopulation.

us.wtpPct.note: 30% -- pre-approval; generic oral antihypertensives ubiquitous; HTA gating to resistant HTN likely.

eu.reachPct.note: 0.8% EU HTN -- EMA post-Ph3 2028+; HTA strict vs generics.

eu.wtpPct.note: 22% -- resistant HTN niche; generic oral baseline.

row.reachPct.note: 0.3% of huge ROW HTN TAM -- Roche global rollout pending Ph3.

row.wtpPct.note: 8% -- Japan NHI pathway if approved, EM generic dominant.

market.notes: RNAi targeting APP for CAA and Alzheimer disease. CNS delivery. Very early but massive optionality.

us.patientsK.note: AHA/NINDS: ~500K US CAA-related ICH risk; cerebral amyloid angiopathy underdiagnosed, MRI SWI detection growing.

eu.patientsK.note: EU stroke registries: ~600K CAA at ICH risk EU27+UK; Boston criteria v2.0 improving detection.

row.patientsK.note: Global: ~3M at-risk CAA; overlaps AD pathology; no approved disease-modifying therapy.

us.wtpPct.note: ~25% - no approved CAA-specific Rx; BP control and antiplatelet management; pipeline anti-amyloid trials ongoing.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~15% - supportive care only; EMA no CAA-specific approvals.

row.wtpPct.note: ~5% - minimal access globally; awareness/MRI availability limiting factor.

us.priceK.note: Current <$1K (antihypertensives generic); pipeline anti-amyloid benchmarks Leqembi $26.5K if approved for CAA.

penPct.note: ~8% peak - no approved disease-modifying; pipeline hinges on anti-amyloid trials in CAA (Biogen, Eisai readouts).

us.reachPct.note: Mivelsiran (ALN-APP) Phase 2 CAA/AD; 3% of ~2M early AD + CAA -- first RNAi for AD, APP silencing; early data 2025.

us.priceK.note: Projected WAC ~$60K/yr -- priced at anti-amyloid class Leqembi $26.5K + RNAi premium.

us.wtpPct.note: 20% -- pre-approval; anti-amyloid mAb class challenging; HTA CAA stroke niche more straightforward.

eu.wtpPct.note: 15% -- HTA pathway strict anti-amyloid post-Kisunla/Leqembi experience.

row.reachPct.note: 0.5% ROW AD/CAA -- Japan NHI pathway projected.

row.wtpPct.note: 6% -- Japan NHI if approved, rest minimal.

us.patientsK.note: Alnylam Givlaari + APF 2024: ~3K US acute hepatic porphyria (AHP: AIP, VP, HCP, ADP); ~1K symptomatic with recurrent attacks.

eu.patientsK.note: Orphanet AHP: ~4K EU (Sweden/Finland AIP clusters, South Africa VP).

row.patientsK.note: Global AHP ~20K prevalence; treatable ROW ~2K symptomatic.

us.wtpPct.note: ~75% -- ultra-orphan; Medicare/commercial cover Givlaari (givosiran) post ENVISION Ph3.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~60% -- EMA approved Givlaari 2020; G-BA/HAS orphan reimbursed.

us.priceK.note: $575K/yr anchored to Alnylam Givlaari (givosiran) WAC 2024 ultra-orphan RNAi pricing.

penPct.note: ~40% peak -- captive recurrent-attack subset within AHP diagnosed pool.

us.reachPct.note: Givlaari 51.5% of ~1K US AHP symptomatic pts -- ultra-rare, near ceiling penetration; monthly SC dosing limits some pts.

us.priceK.note: Givlaari WAC $575K/yr (monthly SC); net ~$500K after outcomes-based contracts + rebates.

us.wtpPct.note: 72% -- Medicare Part B + commercial rare disease coverage; specialty distribution Alnylam; CED outcomes tracking.

eu.reachPct.note: 37% of ~800 EU AHP -- NICE TA639 + G-BA orphan approved; ATU France; specialty porphyria centers.

eu.wtpPct.note: 55% -- NICE/G-BA orphan recommended; managed entry agreements typical.

eu.priceK.note: $320K EU net -- UK/Germany ~55% of US ultra-rare pricing.

row.reachPct.note: 10.3% of ~3K ROW AHP -- Japan NHI 2021, EM minimal access.

row.wtpPct.note: 15% -- Japan NHI rare disease, AU PBS, rest EM access.

row.priceK.note: $120K blended -- Japan NHI ~$250K, EM access program.

us.patientsK.note: Alnylam Oxlumo 10-K + OHF: ~0.4K US PH1 (primary hyperoxaluria type 1, AGXT mutation); ~5K enteric hyperoxaluria secondary.

eu.patientsK.note: Orphanet PH1: ~0.5K EU AGXT-confirmed; ERKNet registry-tracked.

us.wtpPct.note: ~80% -- ultra-orphan kidney stone disease; Medicare/commercial cover Oxlumo (lumasiran) post ILLUMINATE-A Ph3.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~65% -- EMA approved Oxlumo 2020; G-BA/HAS orphan reimbursed.

us.priceK.note: $495K/yr anchored to Alnylam Oxlumo (lumasiran) WAC 2024; nedosiran (Novo/Dicerna) similar tier.

penPct.note: ~50% peak -- ultra-orphan captive pool + prophylaxis prevents ESRD progression.

us.reachPct.note: Oxlumo 95% of ~300 US PH1 pts -- ultra-rare ceiling penetration achieved; only approved PH1 therapy; q3mo SC maint.

us.priceK.note: Oxlumo WAC $495K/yr weight-based; pediatric dosing lower; outcomes-based with LT/KT avoidance value.

us.wtpPct.note: 75% -- Medicare + commercial ultra-rare; specialty distribution; CED reporting; near-universal access for diagnosed.

eu.reachPct.note: 79% of ~400 EU PH1 -- NICE HST17 + G-BA orphan; pediatric metabolism centers gatekeep.

eu.wtpPct.note: 58% -- NICE HST + G-BA recommended ultra-rare; managed entry, weight-based dose.

row.reachPct.note: 22.5% of ~1K ROW PH1 -- Japan NHI 2022, EM access via Alnylam.

mega_bear.narrative: ATTR-CM growth decelerates + zilebesiran CVOT fails + acoramidis competition
events: X Amvuttra growth slows — acoramidis (BridgeBio) takes ATTR-CM share
X Zilebesiran ZENITH CVOT negative — hypertension thesis dead
X Competition from Ionis eplontersen ATTR-CM
-> $43B market cap compresses

bear.narrative: TTR franchise grows but competition limits + hypertension slow
events: V Amvuttra grows to $5-6B but acoramidis splits market
~ Zilebesiran CVOT borderline
~ Nucresiran advances but Amvuttra dominates
-> Revenue $6-7B by 2028

base.narrative: Amvuttra $8B+ peak + zilebesiran validates + Alnylam 2030 on track
events: V Amvuttra $8B+ peak — ATTR-CM standard of care
V 2026 guide $4.9-5.3B met — 71% growth
V Zilebesiran CVOT advancing — hypertension optionality
V Alnylam 2030: 25%+ revenue CAGR target

bull.narrative: RNAi platform dominance — ATTR + hypertension + Alzheimer — $15B+ revenue
events: V Amvuttra $10B+ — dominates ATTR-CM globally
V Zilebesiran CVOT positive — hypertension blockbuster $5B+
V Platform generates 3+ more blockbusters via partnerships
V Revenue $15B+ by 2030

psychedelic_bull.narrative: RNAi becomes the dominant therapeutic modality — Alnylam = Genentech of genetic medicine
events: V ATTR + HTN + Alzheimer + rare = $20B+ platform
V RNAi replaces chronic small molecule therapy across CV, neuro, metabolic
V Roche/Novartis/Regeneron partnerships generate $5B+ in royalties
V Market cap $100B+

lastData.title: $2.99B 2025 rev (+81%), 2026 guide $4.9-5.3B, Alnylam 2030 launched
points: First year of non-GAAP profitability achieved 2025
Zilebesiran ZENITH CVOT with Roche advancing

nextCatalyst.title: Revenue trajectory + nucresiran Phase 3 + zilebesiran CVOT progress

risks: Zilebesiran CVOT is years away — massive investment
Amvuttra pricing pressure as ATTR-CM population expands
RNAi manufacturing at scale is complex
GLP-1 weight loss may reduce CV risk indirectly

catalysts.title: Mivelsiran Phase 2 Alzheimer's disease trial initiation
catalysts._rationale: Company guidance shows planned H1 2026 initiation but no clear confirmation of actual start yet

catalysts.title: ALN-4324 GRB14 diabetes preclinical data presentation
catalysts._rationale: Preclinical data for type 2 diabetes program to be presented in 2026

catalysts.title: ALN-HTT02 Huntington's disease Phase 1b data at medical congress
catalysts._rationale: Company expects to share substantial progress from ALN-HTT02 study at a medical congress in 2026
